[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2015
[TITLE]: Why does this Palm Beach County man chase dangerous storms?
[METADATA/INTRO]: Why does this Palm Beach County man chase dangerous storms? The summer storm season is heating up. The start of hurricane season is just a few weeks away. And Monday, May 4, is National Weather Observers Day....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: “In 2004, he intercepted the eye of slow-moving Hurricane Frances in St. Lucie and Martin counties and rode out Hurricane Jeanne in a downtown Fort Pierce parking garage. The following year, he camped out in a West Palm Beach parking garage to watch Hurricane Wilma batter downtown. 
 “He’s shot wildfires and water spouts, captured telling images of deep freezes and severe droughts. But his favorite subject is the big South Florida thunderstorm, and western Palm Beach County is one of his most reliable backdrops for photographing them. “I love the big landscapes,” he says. 
 “Jeff can capture visually the storm probably better than just about anybody I know,” says Jason Foster of Tampa, who’s chased storms in Tornado Alley with Gammons. “He’s got the eye for it — how to grab it, work it, film it, edit it, present it, share it. There’s guys that take great pictures, but they keep it to themselves. But he lets the world know, and that’s why he has such a great following.”

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2018
[TITLE]: Fake Hurricane Michael viral photo was really shot at Pensacola Beach
[METADATA/INTRO]: Fake viral photo of Hurricane Michael was really shot at Pensacola Beach A viral photo claiming to show Hurricane Michael descending on the Florida Panhandle actually depicts dark clouds rolling over Pensacola Beach last summer, and the photographer behind the...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Fake viral photo of Hurricane Michael was really shot at Pensacola Beach 
 A viral photo claiming to show Hurricane Michael descending on the Florida Panhandle actually depicts dark clouds rolling over Pensacola Beach last summer, and the photographer behind the image wants to set the record straight. 
 Since Michael made landfall on Wednesday afternoon, the photo depicting swirling, menacing clouds overlooking Via de Luna Drive has been shared by thousands of social media users all over the Florida Panhandle and beyond. And many of the users have incorrectly referred to it as a photo of Panama City Beach just before Michael came barreling down.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2015-07-24
[TITLE]: Massive Shelf Cloud sprawled over several counties
[METADATA/INTRO]: Massive Shelf Cloud sprawled over several counties GARDEN CITY, GA (WTOC) - From radar to satellite, our First Alert Weather team helps keep you safe, but your pictures help us tell the story. Meteorologist Jamie Ertle explains the huge ominous...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Massive Shelf Cloud sprawled over several counties 
 GARDEN CITY, GA (WTOC) - From radar to satellite, our First Alert Weather team helps keep you safe, but your pictures help us tell the story. Meteorologist Jamie Ertle explains the huge ominous cloud that seemed to swallow the sky, Thursday afternoon. 
 It looked angry and hungry. It crossed the Savannah River and everyone took notice. The Georgia Port Authority and its massive operations seemed small in comparison to the over-reaching storm cloud. 
 Bunny's title to our email, pics@wtoc.com, was "dark strange clouds" in Garden City. The dark, strange cloud is a shelf cloud aka "arcus" cloud.
